name: OmopConditionConditionOccurrenceView
title: Condition → condition_occurrence (flat)
description: One FHIR Condition maps to one condition_occurrence row. Status filtering drops entered-in-error, refuted, and inactive/resolved conditions. Onset is resolved via a priority chain (onsetDateTime > onsetPeriod.start > recordedDate); conditions with no resolvable start date are skipped. Category drives type_concept_id (problem-list-item vs encounter-diagnosis). Domain routing is not implemented -- all conditions go to condition_occurrence regardless of OMOP vocabulary domain_id.

column.name: condition_occurrence_id
column.description: Surrogate key generated from Condition.id. Hash/sequence/lookup. HL7 IG FML has this commented out (line 11).

column.name: person_id
column.description: Resolve Patient reference to integer. Defaults to 0 if unresolved; omoponfhir throws FHIRException.

column.name: condition_concept_id
column.description: Vocabulary lookup (SNOMED/ICD-10 -> OMOP standard concept). Placeholder: 0. Production requires Athena vocabulary tables. — transform: vocabularyLookup(selectBestCoding(code, [SNOMED, ICD10CM, ICD10, CPT4]))

column.name: condition_start_date
column.description: Falls back through onsetDateTime > onsetPeriod.start > recordedDate. Condition skipped if none resolvable. — transform: coalesce(onsetDateTime, onsetPeriod.start, recordedDate)

column.name: condition_start_datetime
column.description: Full ISO datetime when available. omoponfhir uses 9999-12-31 when missing (line 542). — transform: coalesce(onsetDateTime, onsetPeriod.start, recordedDate)

column.name: condition_end_date
column.description: FhirToCdm falls back to visit end date when absent (line 301-302). No other implementation does this. — transform: coalesce(abatementDateTime, abatementPeriod.end)

column.name: condition_type_concept_id
column.description: problem-list-item->32840, encounter-diagnosis->32817, default->32817. Implementations vary widely.

column.name: condition_status_concept_id
column.description: active/recurrence/relapse->32902, absent->0. ETL-German uses diagnostic confidence instead.

column.name: condition_status_source_value
column.description: Verbatim clinical status code. ETL-German stores diagnostic confidence code here.

column.name: stop_reason
column.description: Free-text reason for resolution. Truncated to 20 chars. fhir-x-omop uses note[0].text instead. — transform: substring(abatementString, 0, 20)

column.name: provider_id
column.description: Asserter preferred; recorder fallback. omoponfhir uses asserter only. fhir-x-omop uses recorder only. — transform: coalesce(resolveRef(asserter), resolveRef(recorder))

column.name: visit_occurrence_id
column.description: Resolve Encounter reference. FhirToCdm also sets visit_detail_id from encounter.

column.name: condition_source_value
column.description: Best code by vocabulary priority. ETL-German stores raw ICD code. — transform: selectBestCoding(code, [SNOMED, ICD10CM, ICD10, CPT4]).code

where.description: clinicalStatus in (active, recurrence, relapse, absent) AND verificationStatus not in (entered-in-error, refuted) AND onset or recordedDate resolvable
